name: Telegram
summary: Cloud-based messaging service that can double as an ad hoc file-sharing method through chat attachments, channels, and public links.

account.benefits: A Telegram account is required. Shared files sync across devices and can be distributed through chats, channels, or public links.

developer.notes: Telegram publishes official file-upload and download API docs, but the consumer sharing workflow is chat-centric rather than a normal CLI file-host flow.

allowed_file_types.notes: Telegram says users can send and receive files of any type up to 2 GB each in regular cloud chats.

security.notes: Regular Telegram cloud chats are not end-to-end encrypted. Telegram treats Secret Chats as a separate, device-specific secure mode.

sources.label: Telegram FAQ
sources.notes: Official FAQ describing Telegram as cloud-based, supporting unlimited sharing of files up to 2 GB each, with Secret Chats as a separate privacy mode.

sources.label: Telegram 2 GB file sharing announcement
sources.notes: Official blog post announcing unlimited file sharing with files up to 2 GB each.

sources.label: Telegram downloads and attachments update
sources.notes: Official update saying users can send files of any type up to 2 GB each and access them from any device with unlimited cloud storage.

sources.label: Telegram file API docs
sources.notes: Official developer documentation for uploading and downloading files through Telegram APIs.

sources.label: Telegram Premium FAQ
sources.notes: Official Premium FAQ listing faster download speed as a paid feature and confirming unlimited cloud storage remains free.

sources.label: Telegram CDN docs
sources.notes: Official infrastructure documentation explaining that popular public files may be delivered from CDN data centers depending on regional traffic and popularity.

primary_use.notes: Telegram is primarily a cloud-based messaging platform that doubles as an ad hoc file-sharing method through conversations, channels, and public posts.

sharing_surface.notes: Shared files can move through private chats, groups, channels, and publicly reachable links.

max_file_size.notes: Telegram says users can send files of any type up to 2 GB each in regular cloud chats.

persistence_model.notes: Files in regular cloud chats remain until deleted by the user or chat participants. Secret Chats are separate and can self-destruct.

storage_model.notes: Telegram positions regular chats as unlimited cloud storage for shared files and media.

bandwidth_model.notes: Conditional: Telegram does not publish a simple file-host-style bandwidth quota. The official Premium FAQ makes faster download speed a paid feature, and Telegram's CDN docs show public-file delivery can depend on infrastructure path and popularity.

name: VKontakte
summary: Social network whose document attachments can also be used as an ad hoc file-sharing method.

account.benefits: A VK account is required to use document attachments as a sharing method inside the social network.

developer.notes: VK has an official developer platform, but this seed entry has not yet mapped a dedicated document-upload workflow in the public docs.

allowed_file_types.notes: Document attachments clearly exist, but detailed current format restrictions still need deeper first-party verification.

security.notes: VK runs over HTTPS, but the public official surfaces used here do not position document attachments as end-to-end encrypted file storage.

sources.label: VKontakte homepage
sources.notes: Official VK homepage confirming the service is an active social network product.

sources.label: VK developer portal
sources.notes: Official VK developer portal confirming an API surface exists.

primary_use.notes: VK document attachments are secondary to the platform's main social-network workflow.

sharing_surface.value: Document attachments in chats and social surfaces
sharing_surface.notes: Files are shared through VK's account-based social and messaging surfaces rather than through a dedicated file-host workflow.

max_file_size.notes: Detailed document-attachment limits on the currently accessible official surfaces still need deeper first-party verification.

persistence_model.notes: The public official surfaces do not currently publish a dedicated retention policy for document attachments used as file sharing.

storage_model.notes: The public official surfaces do not publish a separate storage quota for document attachments used this way.

bandwidth_model.notes: The public official surfaces do not publish a separate bandwidth cap for document attachments.
